description: Loop Over Items with reset:true for polling a long-running job — Wait between checks, status IF with $runIndex ceiling, termination via break-out branch not natural done output.

conversation.text: On form submit, start a background export by POSTing to https://api.example.com/exports, then check every 30 seconds until the job status is complete or you've checked 10 times — whichever comes first. Then grab the download URL from the result.

outcomeExpectations: After starting the export, the workflow polls job status in a loop (Loop Over Items with reset or equivalent) with a delay between checks (around 30 seconds).
Polling terminates when status is complete OR an iteration cap of 10 is reached — explicit stop condition, not an unbounded loop.
The final step reads the download URL from the completed job response.

executionScenarios.name: completes-on-second-poll
executionScenarios.description: Job pending then complete; download URL extracted
executionScenarios.dataSetup: POST https://api.example.com/exports returns { "job_id": "exp-42" }. The first GET https://api.example.com/exports/exp-42/status returns { "status": "pending" }. The second GET https://api.example.com/exports/exp-42/status returns { "status": "complete", "download_url": "https://cdn.example.com/files/report.csv" }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. Status is checked at least twice before succeeding. The workflow output or final step references the download URL https://cdn.example.com/files/report.csv (or the path report.csv). Polling stops after completion — it does not continue indefinitely.
